chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C. P No. D – 2259 of 2024 Hearing of case 1. For orders on office objection at flag ‘A’ 2. For hearing of main case 09.10.2025 Petitioner Shoukat Ali Mahar present in person Mr. Ali Raza Baloch, Additional Advocate General Sindh along with Manzoor Kanrani, Deputy Commissioner, Ghotki and Naveed Ali, Assistant Commissioner, Ghotki Private respondents 12, 14, 15, 17, 18, 21, 25, 28, 29, 30, 33, 37, 39 and 40, namely Shamansuddin, Raza Muhammad, Asghar Ali, Abdul Aziz, Lal Muhammad, Imdad Ali, Ramzan, Ghous Bux, Jan Muhammad, Sadoro, Rahim Dino, Jamaluddin, Arbelo and Abdul Rasheed O R D E R Mr. Imtiaz Ali Malano, Advocate files vakalatnama on behalf of private respondents No.12 and 15. In compliance of order dated 10-09-2025, Deputy Commissioner, Ghotki present in person submits report, which is taken on record. Through the instant Constitutional Petition, the Petitioner seeks directions against the official respondents for removal of alleged encroachments from the graveyard known as Shaheedan Mahar, situated in Village Mehar Mahar, Taluka and District Ghotki. Conversely, Respondent No.28, namely Ghous Bux, present in Court along with others contends that an amount of Rs.44,00,000/- (Rupees Forty-Four Lacs) is outstanding against the father of the Petitioner, which fact has been admitted by the Petitioner present in Court. The private respondents further submit that the instant Petition has been filed with mala fide intent, only to exert undue pressure upon them on account of the said monetary dispute. In view of the admitted position that a sum of Rs.44,00,000/- stands outstanding against the father of the Petitioner in favour of Respondent No.28, we are of the considered view that the Petitioner has not approached this Court with clean hands.

The private respondents further submit that the instant Petition has been filed with mala fide intent, only to exert undue pressure upon them on account of the said monetary dispute. In view of the admitted position that a sum of Rs.44,00,000/- stands outstanding against the father of the Petitioner in favour of Respondent No.28, we are of the considered view that the Petitioner has not approached this Court with clean hands. Accordingly, he is not entitled to any relief as prayed for. Resultantly, the instant Petition stands dismissed, and the interim order dated 10.09.2025 is hereby vacated. Judge Judge ARBROHI
